title: Referential Integrity Limitations
text: Referential Integrity Limitations Overflow Tables Only connectors which set uinfo->osegm to a non-zero value in the Info call can support referential integrity in the DBMS. When uinfo->osegm is set to zero, Uniface uses a concatenated primary key for overflow tables. In that case the relationship between a base table with a compound primary key and its overflow table cannot be maintained by the DBMS. Incompatibility Between Uniface Modes and DDL The SQL used to create the relationships and foreign key rules (Open mode 4) is generated independently from the SQL to create tables (Open mode 2). It is assumed that the relationships and rules can be created by a combination of the following: CREATE TRIGGER TriggerName ALTER TABLE TableName ADD CONSTRAINT Constraint When a particular RDBMS requires that the relationships and foreign key rules are defined as part of the CREATE TABLE statement, it is not possible to implement referential integrity with the interface described in this book. Similarly it is assumed that the relationships and foreign key rules can be dropped independently. ANSI-2 SQL Compliance ANSI-2 SQL-compliant DBMSs allow a constraint or a trigger to be disabled without it being dropped. In the disabled state, disabling has no semantic effect, but the definition of the constraint or of the trigger is retained in the DBMS dictionary. The following state transition diagram for a single constraint models this functionality: The referential integrity implementation of Uniface supports only the states CONSTRAINT NONEXISTENT and CONSTRAINT ENABLED , and supports only the transitions create constraint enabled and drop constraint . Supporting the state CONSTRAINT DISABLED would needlessly introduce complications and RDBMS dependencies (not all DBMSs support disabling constraints or triggers). When using Uniface to manage constraints, do not disable a constraint instead of dropping it, since Uniface regenerates the SQL to create the constraint when it needs to be enabled.
